title: Ctenitis loheri
taxonomy: Ctenitis loheri is classified in the family Polypodiaceae, a large family of ferns commonly known as the polypody ferns. The family Polypodiaceae is a part of the order Polypodiales, which is a group of ferns that have been separated based on their morphological characteristics. This order includes several families of ferns, such as Dryopteridaceae and Thelypteridaceae. Further classification of Ctenitis loheri may also involve the family's genus and species classification.

intro: Ctenitis loheri, also known as Loher's ctenitis, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae, native to the Philippines. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits of anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ctenitis loheri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Ctenitis loheri is commonly prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and capsules, which are used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
